body:
## Launch readiness review — green the launch gate + harden CI/security

Branch: `claude/launch-readiness-review-xwhoa`. Make Dealix launch-ready from every angle that isn't founder-gated, and hand the founder a clean go-live button. Note: the `ci.yml` "Python quality, tests, readiness" job has been **red (cancelled/failure) on `main` for weeks** — this PR does not introduce that; it fixes the largest chunk of it.

### 1. Unblock the official launch gate
`official_launch_verify.sh` returned `FAIL` on one time-bomb test (`test_scope_requested_within_days` hard-coded `2026-05-10`, outside the 14-day window once the date advanced). Fixed to a relative date. → `OFFICIAL_LAUNCH_VERDICT=PASS` locally (company_ready + GTM stack + frontend build + apps/web build all PASS).

### 2. Security gates (were silently red on every PR)
- **Gitleaks**: replaced licensed `gitleaks-action@v2` with the free OSS binary. It then surfaced 16+3 findings, all reviewed: 16 fake keys in `tests/` (allowlisted `tests/.*`), 3 confirmed non-secrets in `.gitleaksignore` (TTL math; `curl -u "${MOYASAR_SECRET_KEY}:"` env-var refs). → green.
- **Trivy**: `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0/0.30.0` tags stopped resolving → switched to the official install script. → green.

### 3. Next.js security upgrade — 15.1.3 → 15.5.19
`frontend` + `apps/web` (+ `eslint-config-next`), lockfiles regenerated, both rebuild clean. Clears the Trivy Next.js DoS advisories (incl. GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3).

### 4. CI test job — biggest cluster fixed + timeout fixed
- **hermes (64 failures → 0)**: `tests/test_hermes_system.py` used the deprecated `asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete()` (70×). Under xdist, a prior test leaves the loop policy with `_set_called=True, _loop=None`, so it raised "no current event loop" for these sync tests (they passed in isolation). Switched to `asyncio.run()`. Full-suite failures **120 → 55**.
- **35-min job timeout**: a few DB-dependent tests hang on `postgres:5432` and consumed the whole budget → job was *cancelled* with no report. Added `pytest-timeout` + `--timeout=120 --timeout-method=thread` so hangs fail fast and the job completes with a bounded, actionable result.

### Remaining: 55 pre-existing test failures (tracked backlog — NOT introduced here)
Verified as individual real failures (no systemic fix; each passes/fails on its own logic): DB-dependent (postgres + real assertion drift), subprocess verify-scripts, content/policy scans, and ~14 misc. **2 categories are founder decisions, deliberately not "fixed to green":**
- **Auth (security policy)**: `api/security/api_key.py` allows requests through when `API_KEYS` is unset ("dev mode"), regardless of `APP_ENV`. `test_empty_keys_env_blocks_all` expects fail-closed in production. → decide: harden code to fail-closed, or update the test.
- **Pricing**: `test_billing_amounts` expects 999 SAR (code says 2999) and `>0` for the free `free_mini_diagnostic` (legitimately 0). → confirm intended prices.

### Founder-gated (Dealix doctrine — not in scope)
GitHub secrets · Railway Moyasar **live** + webhook · real CRM KPIs · warm-list names · first 10 approved touches. No external sends / live charging / fake data introduced. Ref: `docs/company/DEALIX_COMPANY_READY_MASTER_AR.md`.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016uVzUEgs4o9jhuP8Gmhv3v

commits.messageBody:
The test hard-coded event_date 2026-05-10, which fell outside the
14-day window once the date advanced, breaking the official launch
gate (official_launch_verify.sh -> company_ready -> commercial pytest).
Use a date relative to today so the assertion never goes stale.

Unblocks OFFICIAL_LAUNCH_VERDICT=PASS.

- Gitleaks: gitleaks-action@v2 hard-fails for GitHub orgs without a paid
  GITLEAKS_LICENSE secret. Replace with the free open-source gitleaks
  binary (pinned 8.30.1), scanning the working tree against .gitleaks.toml.
- Trivy: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0 tag no longer resolves on the
  marketplace. Bump to @0.30.0.

Both jobs were red on every PR and on main, independent of code changes.
commits.messageHeadline: ci(hardening): fix pre-existing Gitleaks + Trivy launch-gate failures

commits.messageBody:
- gitleaks: add --verbose so any finding is visible (rule + file:line,
  value redacted) for triage.
- Trivy: install via official aquasecurity install script instead of the
  marketplace action, whose 0.24.0/0.30.0 tags no longer resolve on GitHub.
commits.messageHeadline: ci(hardening): verbose gitleaks + robust Trivy install

commits.messageBody:
Trivy (now functional) flagged real Next.js DoS advisories
(GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3 et al.) in 15.1.3. Bump Next.js 15.1.3 -> 15.5.19
in frontend and apps/web (+ eslint-config-next), regenerate lockfiles.
Both apps rebuild clean (next build exit 0).

Gitleaks: all 16 findings were fake sample keys inside tests/ (including
tests of the secret-scanner itself). Broaden the allowlist path from
tests/fixtures/.* to tests/.* — no real secrets in tracked code.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(security): clear Trivy + Gitleaks launch-gate findings

commits.messageBody:
After scoping tests/ out, gitleaks flagged 3 remaining findings, all
manually verified as non-secrets:
- api/routers/pricing.py:250  -> TTL math (30 * 86400), not a key
- docs/ops/moyasar_live_test.sh:40 & scripts/ops/moyasar_pilot_test.sh:103
  -> curl -u "${MOYASAR_SECRET_KEY}:" env-var refs, not hard-coded keys

Record their fingerprints in .gitleaksignore so the scan stays green
without weakening detection elsewhere.
commits.messageHeadline: ci(hardening): ignore 3 reviewed gitleaks false positives

commits.messageBody:
tests/test_hermes_system.py called
asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(...) (70x). Under xdist
parallel runs, a prior test on the same worker leaves the event-loop
policy with _set_called=True and _loop=None, so get_event_loop() raises
'There is no current event loop' for these sync tests -> 64 failures in
the full suite (they passed in isolation). asyncio.run() creates a fresh
loop each call and is robust to that cross-test contamination.
commits.messageHeadline: test(hermes): use asyncio.run instead of deprecated get_event_loop

commits.messageBody:
The 'Python quality, tests, readiness' job was hitting its 35-min
timeout-minutes and getting cancelled (no report, no coverage). Root
cause: a handful of DB-dependent tests try to connect to postgres
(127.0.0.1:5432) and hang until the job-level timeout, consuming the
whole budget. Add pytest-timeout with --timeout=120 --timeout-method=thread
so a hanging test fails fast and the job completes with a bounded,
actionable result instead of being cancelled.
commits.messageHeadline: ci: add per-test timeout to the coverage-gate test run

commits.messageBody:
The full ~4.7k-test suite + coverage takes ~33-35 min on the 2-core CI
runners — right at the old 35-min edge: one run completed at 34.5 min,
a slightly slower one was cancelled at 35 min. Combined with the per-test
timeout (which kills hangs), 50 min gives the job comfortable headroom to
always complete and report results instead of being cancelled.
commits.messageHeadline: ci: raise test-job timeout 35->50 min for reliable completion

title: Launch readiness: green the official launch gate + security/CI hardening
